Review of Merchant Card Payment Costs and Surcharging – Conclusions Paper
speeches.summary: Media release number 2026-10: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has today published a Conclusions Paper which sets out the final decisions of the Payments System Board (PSB) on the Review of Merchant Card Payment Costs and Surcharging.

speeches.title: Changes to Bank of Canada Governing Council
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada announced today the departure of Deputy Governor Rhys Mendes on April 10, and the retirement of Deputy Governor Sharon Kozicki on July 15.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada maintains policy rate at 2¼%
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today held its target for the overnight rate at 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada, Export Development Canada, RBC and TD successfully complete bond issuance experiment  using distributed ledger technology
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada (BoC), RBC Capital Markets, RBC Investor Services, TD Bank Group (TD), and Export Development Canada (EDC) successfully completed Project Samara, a collaborative initiative to evaluate how tokenization and distributed ledger technology (DLT) can improve bond issuance and settlement in a real-world setting.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada issues revised compliance order to XTM Inc.
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today issued a revised temporary order to XTM Inc. (XTM). The Bank has authorized XTM to recommence retail payment activities under the supervision of the Monitor appointed today by the Ontario Superior Court of Justice under the Companies' Creditors Arrangement Act, and subject to terms and conditions set out in the Bank's revised order.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada issues immediate compliance order to XTM Inc.
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today issued a temporary order to XTM Inc. (XTM) to immediately cease performing any retail payment activities.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada maintains policy rate at 2¼%
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today held its target for the overnight rate at 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada maintains policy rate at 2¼%
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today held its target for the overnight rate at 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada announces finalists for the eleventh annual Governor’s Challenge
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada has announced the five finalist teams in the 2025-26 edition of The Governor’s Challenge, a competition where university students simulate the role of advisor to the Bank’s Governing Council.

speeches.title: Bank of Canada lowers policy rate to 2¼%
speeches.summary: The Bank of Canada today reduced its target for the overnight rate by 25 basis points to 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and the deposit rate at 2.20%.

speeches.title: Triennial Central Bank Survey of Foreign Exchange and Derivatives Market Activity in Canada during April 2025
speeches.summary: In April, the Bank of Canada and 51 other central banks and monetary authorities conducted the latest triennial survey of turnover activity in the foreign exchange (FX) and over-the-counter (OTC) derivatives markets in their respective countries.

items.headline: EasyJet Shares Slump Amid Warning of Loss Driven by Iran War
items.summary: EasyJet Plc shares fell the most since 2022 after the UK budget airline warned of a loss in the first half of this fiscal year because of disruptions from the Iran war.

items.headline: Sigenergy Jumps 103% in Hong Kong Debut After $562 Million IPO
items.summary: Shares of Sigenergy Technology Co., a Chinese maker of energy storage equipment, jumped in their Hong Kong trading debut Thursday after the company raised HK$4.4 billion ($562 million) in an initial public offering, a sign of investor demand in an industry swept up in a frenzied rally.

items.headline: TotalEnergies Flags Strong Quarter as War Rattles Energy Market
items.summary: TotalEnergies SE signaled a strong first quarter as surging energy prices, trading gains and rising production elsewhere helped offset the impact of the war on its Middle East operations.

items.headline: Brad Setser on the War in Iran and the Future of the US Dollar
items.summary: How This Shock Differs From the 1970s.

items.headline: Odd Lots: Setser on the Future of the US Dollar (Podcast)
Barry Callebaut Drops Most in a Year After Profit Outlook Cut
items.summary: Swiss chocolate maker Barry Callebaut AG said profit will decline this year, cutting its outlook after a sharp drop in cocoa prices.

items.headline: SoftBank Pays Record 8.5% on Part of $3.6 Billion Bond Sale
items.summary: SoftBank Group Corp. agreed to pay its highest interest rate on dollar notes as part of a $3.6 billion fundraising, underscoring concerns about the Japanese conglomerate’s large-scale investments in artificial intelligence.

items.headline: Won't Be Seeing Three ECB Rate Hikes in 2026, BlackRock Says
items.summary: James Turner, BlackRock global fixed income EMEA head, discusses the European economy and bond space in light of the current geopolitical situation in the Middle East and its impact on supply chains. "We have to remember that it's very much a supply-side shock," Turner tells Bloomberg Television. He also talks about the European Central Bank and says it is unlikely the ECB will deliver three rate hikes in 2026. "I don't think, for example, we're going to see three rate rises from the ECB this year. I am not even sure we'll see two." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: ECB Officials Are Leaning Toward April Rate Hold
items.summary: Policymakers at the European Central Bank are leaning toward keeping interest rates unchanged this month, postponing their verdict on whether the fallout of the Iran war warrants a response, according to people familiar with the debate.

items.headline: Stellantis Invests €100 Million to Keep Site Near Paris Open
items.summary: Stellantis NV set plans to invest €100 million ($118 million) to transform its Poissy car-assembly plant near Paris, an initiative to keep the site open as the maker Peugeots and Citroëns scales back its European manufacturing operations.

items.headline: Korean Air Up on Earnings Surprise Despite Fuel Fears
items.summary: Korean Air Lines Co. shares climbed after South Korea’s largest flag carrier delivered a first-quarter earnings surprise, defying mounting concerns about jet fuel shortages and surging costs amid the Middle East crisis.

items.headline: Stocks Extend Highs as TSMC Bolsters Tech Optimism: Markets Wrap
items.summary: Stocks set new highs as an upbeat forecast from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. added fuel to technology shares amid rising optimism that the US and Iran are seeking more time to negotiate a peace deal.

items.headline: Evergrande Liquidators Said to Pick State-Owned Tourism Firm for Exclusive Sale Talks
items.summary: China Evergrande Group’s liquidators have selected Guangdong Provincial Tourism Holdings Co. to enter exclusive talks to acquire a majority stake in the developer’s property management unit, people familiar with the situation said, as they try to claw back some money from one of the country’s most significant corporate collapses.

items.headline: Inditex Flags Contractor Data Leak, Says Client Records Safe
items.summary: Zara owner Inditex SA disclosed a data breach at an outside firm, saying intruders gained access to information on commercial relations at the world’s biggest clothing firm.

items.headline: Tesco Says Iran War Is Clouding Outlook for UK Shoppers
items.summary: Tesco Plc said the conflict in the Middle East may weigh on its performance, with profit potentially coming in slightly lower this fiscal year depending on the ripple effects of the war on consumer spending.

items.headline: Indian LNG Importers Scoop Up Spot Shipments After Prices Recede
items.summary: India’s liquefied natural gas importers have accelerated purchases from the spot market, taking advantage of a recent dip in prices, as the country looks to ease a supply crunch triggered by the war in the Middle East.

items.headline: China’s Economy Rides Out War as Growth Unexpectedly Touches 5%
items.summary: China’s economic growth rebounded more than expected in the first quarter, suggesting limited spillovers so far from the war in Iran but revealing few signs of turnaround in weak consumer spending.

items.headline: Global Investors Boost Dollar-Hedging Ratio to Two-Year High
items.summary: International investors are boosting their hedges against dollar declines as the US-Iran ceasefire erodes the haven demand that had bolstered the greenback while the fighting raged on.

items.headline: UK Bonds Rise After Economy Grew Pre-Iran War
Singapore Rejects US Claims in Manufacturing, Forced Labor Probe
items.summary: Singapore’s business lobby has joined the government in pushing back against a sweeping US trade probe that alleges manufacturing overcapacity and failures to enforce bans on imports linked to forced labor.

items.headline: Abu Dhabi Fund Led by Crown Prince Inks $2.3 Billion Jordan Deal
items.summary: A newly created wealth fund overseen by Abu Dhabi’s crown prince has signed a $2.3 billion pact for a railway project in Jordan, highlighting the role the entity is likely to play in advancing the emirate’s strategic relationships.

items.headline: Kganyago Cites Zulu War to Signal Preemptive Inflation Move
items.summary: South Africa’s central bank governor referenced an 1879 battle in the Anglo-Zulu war in his views on how to tackle the coming wave on inflation from the Iran war shocks, implying a preference to raise interest rates sooner rather than later.

items.headline: Japan’s Nikkei Closes at Record High, Wiping Out Iran War Losses
items.summary: Japan’s Nikkei 225 Stock Average ended the day at a new record high, erasing its losses from the Iran war on optimism the new peace talks may hasten the end to the conflict.

items.headline: CATL Overtakes PetroChina in Market Value as Green Bets Ramp Up
items.summary: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. has once again overtaken oil giant PetroChina Co. in market value, as investors pile into the battery maker on war-driven optimism over green energy.

items.headline: Investors Look Past War to Focus on Fundamentals, Yardeni Says
items.summary: Wall Street veteran Ed Yardeni said investors are looking past the war in the Middle East and focusing on market fundamentals.

items.headline: Chinese Economy Blows Past Growth Forecasts Despite Mideast War | The China Show 4/16/2026
items.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, David Ingles and Yvonne Man give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Horizons Middle East & Africa 4/16/2026
items.summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Paschi Investors Back Fresh Term for Ousted CEO Lovaglio
items.summary: Monte dei Paschi investors backed another term for ousted Chief Executive Officer Luigi Lovaglio, marking a victory for the veteran leader after the board tried to get rid of him. Bloomberg's Chiara Albanese has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Orban’s Defeat Ignites Euro Convergence Trade in Hungary
items.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s election defeat is extending a historic rally in Hungary’s bond market as his successor dangles the prospects of the country adopting the euro.

items.headline: Oil Steady on Signs US, Iran Working Toward Ceasefire Extension
items.summary: Oil steadied on signs the US and Iran may extend a ceasefire and restart talks to end the war that’s rocked energy markets.

events.event_name: U.S. dollar seen strengthening as U.S.-Israel strikes intensify
OPEC+ agrees modest oil output boost even as US war on Iran disrupts shipments
